Your Healing Kitchen
Natural healing · Fourth trimester
Morning tonic • Postpartum recovery • On waking

Morning Waters

Three simple warm-water sips to start the day — pick your water by how you feel.
Makes 1 cup
Time 2 min
Caffeine none
For you · on waking
When to drink On waking, on an empty stomach — 20–30 min before food. Warm, not iced.

Why this heals you

  • Amla — one of the richest vitamin-C sources; unlocks iron, immunity, hair verified ×2
  • Ajwain — a traditional digestive; eases bloating tradition
  • Warm water, empty stomach — gentle on a healing gut; wakes digestion tradition
  • Caffeine-free — a calm start to the day tradition
Ingredients
½ tspAmla powder (vitamin C)
½ tspAjwain seeds
½Lemon
1 cupWarm filtered water
optionalHoney (to taste)
Pick your water
1
Amla water

Stir ½ tsp amla into a cup of warm water. Vitamin C to unlock iron — best with or before an iron meal.

2
Ajwain water

Steep ½ tsp ajwain seeds in hot water 5 min, then strain. For bloating or sluggish digestion.

3
Lemon water

½ lemon squeezed into warm water. Hydration + a gentle wake-up.

Amla is the powerhouse

½ tsp amla is one of the richest vitamin-C hits there is — taken with an iron meal it can lift iron absorption up to ~3×. A little honey if you want it gentler.

Good to know
Bloated?Reach for the ajwain water.
Iron day?Amla water with breakfast.
Why we say this
Amla’s vitamin-C density (and vitamin C’s iron-absorption boost) is corroborated by ≥2 dated sources; ajwain-for-digestion is honestly labelled tradition.
